twitch_info.title: NEW ADVENTURE (fray)!!!! sadly no suggestions today most likely

message.body: 👋elo
im doing pretty good, made some good progress on my fangame project and ready to be doing some sprites. Hoy sre you doing?
Id say its an adventure with a gimmick involving a sword made in reloaded k engine
Pogo, sideways bounce when slashing at a spike and bounce when dashing straight at a spike
And its gonna be mostly platforming
Hmmmm
Maybe I can create wooden spikes to prevent exploiting pogo but I'll figure it out as I go along
Since its my first game lol
I think I'll do art for player and other characters while the tileset I might rip from somewhere else
I def draw using the kid sprite as a reference and have some design ideas in mind
Dunno how its gonna turn out since I havent done much art in my life
Im considering do some voice work but that'll much later
I saw you stream it, its absloutely amazing, I love it
I can confidentaly say I can do silly voices
As for the bosses, I'll just make them into jokes since i dont have any ideas for them and also i dont have the programming abillities
this isnt mewgenics..

normally that type of game isnt for me but i probably check it out more

dyslexia is an umbrella term for any form of reading or writing difficulties, usually taking the form of transpositions (swapping letters or words) or changes (adding or removing letters or words)
hey when i corrected my teachers they would just make fun of me so thats p nice
it ranges from small unimportant things (forgetting a non important word) to big debilitating things (being unable to get through a sentence)

when the teacher asks what my name is and chatgpt servers are down BigSad BigSad BigSad

I'm not a teacher in the traditional sense but I typically respond "the point isn't to know, the point is to want to find out. find something you don't know and want to find out, and then these tools can help you find out"
i did after school tutoring for some people in highschool and did voice training for some people in college, I was never good at either I'm just not a good conveyor of ideas

math is hard to convince people on because basic math is useful for a bunch of nothing stuff, then the middle section of math only exists so that the actual useful math that you learn 7 years later is actually understandable
the point isn't to know that 5*10 is 50, the point is to want to find out how many slices of ham you would need to use to make 10 sandwiches with 5 slices each
